news.headline: Bitcoin ETF inflows soar to $2.2B as Trump's rising election odds create optimism for Republicans, favoring the digital asset sector. BTC gains $2.13B with total crypto AUM nearing $100B. Trading volumes up 30% as market eyes potential price surge.
news.summary: Last week, digital asset investment products experienced a significant increase with inflows surging to $2.2 billion, the largest weekly jump since July 2023. This spike is believed to be driven by optimism surrounding a potential Republican win in the upcoming U.S. presidential election, as Republicans are seen as more supportive of the digital asset sector. Bitcoin recorded $2.13 billion in inflows, contributing to a total assets under management in the crypto market nearing $100 billion. Trading volumes for digital asset investment products also rose by 30%. Pedro Lapenta, Head of Research at Hashdex, highlighted the market's closeness to BTC's all-time high and suggested that historical trends indicate potential explosive price movements in the near future. Ethereum, the second-largest cryptocurrency, also saw...

news.headline: CHINT Group attends 2024 Ou River Summit in Wenzhou with global participants to focus on green energy and power systems.
news.summary: In Wenzhou, China, CHINT Group participated in the 2024 Ou River Summit held from October 18 to 19 with the theme “Green Forward.” The event brought together government officials, scientists, entrepreneurs, experts, scholars, international organizations, and investors globally to explore innovative strategies and advanced technologies for enhancing sustainability and implementing smart solutions in the electric power and green energy sectors. The summit aimed to foster discussions and collaborations towards the development of a more environmentally-friendly and efficient energy landscape.

news.headline: Whale trader buys $7.14M worth of GOAT tokens, pocketing $3M profit. Another sells 9.25M, fuels crypto hype.
news.summary: A significant development in the crypto world involves whale activity linked to the AI-generated GOAT meme coin, capturing attention and prompting a rebound from a brief decline in Monday trading. Lookonchain data reveals one whale investor's notable accumulation of GOAT tokens, purchasing 21.39 million tokens for $7.14 million at an average price of $0.3338 across four wallets in the last four days. This buying trend persists as the whale recently transferred an extra 2 million USDC from Binance to procure more GOAT. Conversely, another whale has been offloading their GOAT holdings after initially investing 602.64 Solana to acquire 10.71 million GOAT tokens. Noteworthy is the sale of 9.25 million tokens by this investor. For further details, the complete story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: ECB paper suggests Bitcoin benefits early adopters at the expense of latecomers, fails as a payment system.
news.summary: The latest ECB report has caused a stir as it suggests that the adoption of Bitcoin may lead to a redistribution of wealth from latecomers to early investors. The paper, titled “The distributional consequences of Bitcoin,” argues that even if Bitcoin’s price continues to rise indefinitely, only early adopters stand to benefit while latecomers and non-holders may face significant losses. Authors Ulrich Bindseil and Jürgen Schaaf claim that Bitcoin has deviated from its original purpose as a global payment system and has now been positioned mainly as an investment asset. They highlight that unlike traditional assets such as real estate, bonds, or stocks, Bitcoin does not generate cash flow, interest, or dividends, making it challenging to apply traditional valuation methods to assess its worth. The economists further suggest that Bitcoin’s potential for perpetual price increases favor early adopters over those entering the market later.

The report provides analysis up to Q3 2028 and is available on ResearchAndMarkets.com for those interested in the market trends and forecasts in Vietnam.

news.headline: LexisNexis® Risk Solutions announced as Nacha's exclusive Preferred Partner for Official Registrar of Routing Numbers, enhancing ACH Network efficiency.
news.summary: A company in Reston, Virginia, Nacha, has announced LexisNexis® Risk Solutions as their exclusive Preferred Partner for Official Registrar of Routing Numbers. LexisNexis® Risk Solutions will provide comprehensive and current routing data to help improve the efficiency and reliability of the ACH Network. This partnership aims to ensure accurate ACH payment routing through the availability and utilization of precise routing information. Nacha continues to collaborate with various organizations to enhance the network's operations, with this latest partnership highlighting the importance of reliable routing data.
